Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

In particular, the "-ffff" part is x86 specific, and possibly wrong now that kernel pointers are non-grata outside the kernel.

Patch Set 1:

Is ffff for kernel pointers x86 specific?  I thought ARM was the same, with kernel pointers in just the upper part of the address space?  (Perhaps Shaun knows for sure?)  Obviously that *is* possibly architecture specific, I just think it might be true on both archs of interest.  (But I've never really wrangled an ARM kernel dump, so...)

My thought was that maybe this is failing when something is a little strange in the config, like an OST is offline - because ost1 should almost always be OST0000.  It's not that using OST0000 rather than the import name is correct - it's obviously not - but it's a little hard to see what would cause it to fail.

parents.subject: LU-16834 obdfilter: Do not attach device if already present

commit.subject: LU-16838 tests: use import name in 398a
commit.message:
LU-16838 tests: use import name in 398a

The LU-15670 test change assumes ost1_import is always
OST0000.  This isn't quite always true, so the test is
failing in certain configurations.

Change it to use the import name.

Fixes: 649d638467 ("LU-15670 clio: Disable lockless for DIO with O_APPEND")
Test-parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ifaefc503d1118ecd6fd45b661cbe94607f7ad799
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51064
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
